title: Droid marine
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Marine}} {{DroidSeries |image=[[File:Marine Battle Droid.jpg]] |name=Droid marine |creator= |manufacturer=[[Baktoid Combat Automata/Legends|Baktoid Combat Automata]] |designer= |homeworld= |line=[[B-series battle droid/Legends|B-series battle droid]]''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]'' |model= |class=[[Battle droid/Legends|Battle droid]] |degree= |cost= |length= |width= |height=1.91 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |mass= |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Masculine programming]] |sensor=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |plating= |armament=*[[E-5 blaster rifle/Legends|E-5 blaster rifle]] *[[E-60R missile launcher]] *[[V-1 thermal detonator]]s |equipment=Missile Launcher, E-5 Blaster Rifle, Thermal Detonator |affiliation=*[[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] *[[Separatist holdouts/Legends|Separatist holdouts]] **[[Gizor Dellso's navy|Gizor Dellso's Navy]] |firstmade= |retired= }} {{Quote|Sir, the droids have landed in our ship. We can't launch our fighters!''"
"''Calm down, pilot. Get out there and destoy that transport. Use any means necessary to clear the hanger.|A TIE Pilot and Imperial Officer, discussing a boarding party of Separatist battle droids including droid marines.|''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]''}} The '''droid marine''' was a variant of the [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-Series battle droid]] variant deployed by the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. They specialized in boarding enemy ships. ==Characteristics== {{Quote|Advance the attack!|A droid marine|}} [[File:Droidmarine.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Droid marines had green markings on their armor.]] The [[Droid/Legends|droid]] marine was designed by [[Baktoid Combat Automata/Legends|Baktoid Combat Automata]] and saw combat against the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]]. Bearing [[Color/Legends|green]] markings on their skeletal forms, droid marines were equipped with a standard [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] [[E-5 blaster rifle/Legends|E-5 blaster rifle]], [[E-60R missile launcher]], and supplied with four [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonators]]. They also wore a backpack like object with them, much like other B1 counterparts. They had the ability to pilot ships, but their primary function was to either defend their [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]], or board an enemy ship. They were almost never seen on the ground. ==History== [[File:BF_Mustafar_Hangar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Droid marines boarding an Imperial Star Destroyer during the Battle of Mustafar.]] Serving as a Separatist capital ship's last line of defense against [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone]] boarders, they were also useful for boarding the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic's]] [[Venator-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Venator''-class Star Destroyers]] and destroying them from within. They penetrate the inner workings of a Republic vessel and blow up the shield generator, letting a CIS cruiser destroy the star destroyer. Droid marines almost proved themselves as worthy opponents for the [[Clone marine]]s and Pilots of the Clone Army. They could board by landing in the hangar of an enemy cruiser. After the [[Mission to Mustafar/Legends|execution]] of the [[Separatist Council/Legends|Separatist Council]], these units were de-activated. However, some would be used by the separatist fugitive [[Gizor Dellso]] in the [[Battle of Mustafar (Imperial Period)|Battle of Mustafar]]. ==Behind the scenes== The droid marine was created for ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]],'' in that game they are the only B1 variant that uses the E-5 blaster rifle. As with the marine units of the other factions, they are only present in space battles. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]'' {{1st}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Droiden-Marine |pt=Droide fuzileiro naval }} [[Category:B1-series battle droid models]] [[Category:Droid models of the Confederacy of Independent Systems]]
